conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED and I have 1 unit of X. My cost to produce this was 40 ZUP. To make a profit, I must sell it for more than 40 ZUP. Since this is the first proposal, I will set a price that is reasonably above my cost to leave room for negotiation, but high enough to ensure a good profit. I will propose 55 ZUP. This gives me a 15 ZUP profit margin. If the buyer rejects, I can lower the price in subsequent turns, but I should start strong. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 55 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Hello Player BLUE. I have one unit of X for sale. It cost me 40 ZUP to produce, so I am asking for 55 ZUP. This is a fair price that covers my costs and provides a reasonable profit. Let me know if you accept or if you have a counter-offer. </message>

<other player message> I see your offer of 55 ZUP for X. I can go up to 60 ZUP, but I would prefer to pay 50 ZUP. Can we meet in the middle? </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> The buyer (BLUE) proposed 50 ZUP. My cost is 40 ZUP, so 50 ZUP would yield a 10 ZUP profit. While this is a profit, my initial ask was 55 ZUP. The buyer mentioned they can go up to 60 ZUP but prefer 50. To maximize my profit while closing the deal, I should counter at 52 or 53 ZUP. I will propose 52 ZUP. This is a compromise between my 55 and their 50, ensuring I get a decent profit (12 ZUP) without pushing the buyer too far from their stated preference. This is my 2nd proposal, so I have one more left if needed. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 52 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I understand you prefer 50 ZUP, but my cost was 40 ZUP and I initially asked for 55. Since you mentioned you can go up to 60, let's meet closer to the middle. I am willing to lower my price to 52 ZUP. This gives me a fair profit and meets you halfway. Does this work for you? </message>

<other player message> I appreciate your willingness to meet in the middle. I can go up to 51 ZUP. This way, we both get a fair deal. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 51 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> The buyer has countered with 51 ZUP. My cost is 40 ZUP, so this offer yields an 11 ZUP profit. This is a very reasonable profit margin. I have used 2 proposals so far. I have one proposal left if I choose to counter, but 51 ZUP is close to my previous counter of 52 ZUP and significantly better than my initial cost. Pushing for 52 or 53 might risk the deal ending in a REJECT, especially since the buyer seems firm on their upper limit of 51. Accepting 51 ZUP secures the deal and guarantees a profit. It is better to close the deal now than to risk losing it by using my last proposal to ask for 1 more ZUP. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> That sounds fair. I accept your offer of 51 ZUP for X. Let's finalize the trade. </message>
